resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(self, s: str) -> int:
        last_seen = {}
        max_length = -1
        for i, c in enumerate(s):
            if c in last_seen:
                max_length = max(max_length, i - last_seen[c] - 1)
            last_seen[c] = i
        return max_length
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: This solution uses a dictionary to store the last seen index of each character. It then iterates over the string, updating the maximum length of the substring between equal characters. The time complexity is O(n) as it makes a single pass over the string, and the space complexity is O(n) as it stores the last seen index of each character.
